input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
Primary topic: debut singles/albums and songwriting credits for named artists in the passages.

Facts supported verbatim by the passages:
- Evans Blue — "'Cold (But I'm Still Here)' is the first single off Evans Blue's debut album, 'The Melody and the Energetic Nature of Volume'"; "The song was released December 13, 2005."
- Melanie Martinez — "'Dollhouse' is the debut single by American recording artist Melanie Martinez"; "'Dollhouse' ... is the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote"; featured on debut studio album "'Cry Baby' (2015)" and EP "'Dollhouse' (2014)"; lead single released "February 9, 2014."
- Foster & Lloyd — "'Crazy Over You' is a debut song recorded by ... Foster & Lloyd, who also wrote the song"; released "May 1987" as first single from their self-titled debut album.
- Esmée Denters — "'Outta Here' is the title song of the debut studio album by Dutch R&B singer-songwriter Esmée Denters"; released "14 April 2009."
- Agnes — "'Kick Back Relax' ... was written by Jörgen Elofsson who also wrote her debut single 'Right Here Right Now'"; from second album "'Stronger'."
- Gabriela — "Her self-titled debut album was released in 2001; it was produced by Vehnee Saturno, who also wrote eight of the album's ten songs."
- Sammy Kershaw — "'Anywhere but Here' ... released in September 1992 as the final single from his debut album, 'Don't Go Near the Water'"; "co-written by Bob DiPiero and John Scott Sherrill with Buddy Cannon."

Ambiguities / missing information blocking the question:
- No passage states an album titled exactly "I'm Still Here" — MISSING: the artist (bridge entity) who released an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- Passage [1] contains "'Cold (But I'm Still Here)'" (Evans Blue) — this is a similarly worded song title but not an album titled "I'm Still Here" (potential false lead).

Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives:
1) Search/fetch the Wikipedia disambiguation or article for "I'm Still Here (album)" (query: "I'm Still Here (album) - Wikipedia"); inspect disambiguation candidates.
2) For the identified artist page (once found), fetch "Artist Name - Wikipedia" and inspect lede/infobox and the album page "I'm Still Here (Artist Name album)" (sections: Track listing, Singles, Personnel/Credits).
3) If no album page, search for "I'm Still Here (album) 'album' site:wikipedia.org" and check pages/sections for songwriting or single credits (look for "Singles", "Track listing", "Songwriting", "Credits").
4) If multiple artists share the album title, fetch each candidate artist's debut album page and check their debut album's lead single and songwriting credit (sections: "Singles", "Track listing", "Personnel").

input_dict.current_prompt:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.

retrieved_doc_snippets: I'm Still Here (album) | I'm Still Here is the fifth and final studio album by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on March 23, 2010, via Linus Entertainment. The album includes the single "I'm Still Here," as well as re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels." Mindy performed on "Fox & Friends" to promote the album in April 2010. This is McCready's final release before her death in 2013.

You're Still Here | "You're Still Here" is a song recorded by American country music artist Faith Hill. It was released in June 2003 as the fourth single from her fifth album "Cry" (2002). The song reached #28 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart. The song was written by Matraca Berg and Aimee Mayo.
I'm Still Here... Damn It! | I'm Still Here... Damn It is an album released by singer/comedian/actress Sandra Bernhard. The album is a live recording of her one-woman show of the same title;

Cold (But I'm Still Here) | "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" is the first single off Evans Blue's debut album, "The Melody and the Energetic Nature of Volume". The song was released December 13, 2005, two months prior to the album release, and garnered frequent radio play in anticipation of the upcoming album. Evans Blue's official MySpace explains the meaning of this song as well as others by the group. A music video was also produced for "Cold (But I'm Still Here)." The song currently has over thre ...[truncated]

Still Here (John Mark Nelson album) | Still Here is the first full-length album released by John Mark Nelson on 29 December 2011. The songs for this album were crafted when Nelson was 17 years of age. The album was featured on the local radio stations of Minnesota. After this album, Nelson decided to stop his music career for a short time.
I'm Still Here (Mandisa song) | "I'm Still Here" is the second single, released in 2017, from Mandisa's fifth studio album, "Out Of The Dark".

Heart Beats (Dami Im album) | Heart Beats is the second studio album by Australian recording artist Dami Im, released on 14 October 2014 by Sony Music Australia. While Im's previous self-titled debut album consisted of cover songs she performed on "The X Factor Australia", "Heart Beats" features a mixture of original ballads and up-tempo pop songs. Im worked on the album with many songwriters and producers, including Robby De Sa, Jon Hume, Christian Lo Russo, Louis Schoorl, DNA Songs, Trinity an ...[truncated]
source_state.summary_2: The artist who released I'm Still Here is Mindy McCready. The provided passages note McCready's 2010 album I'm Still Here includes the single "I'm Still Here" and re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels," but they do not state who wrote the debut song on her 1996 debut album.
source_state.answer: I can’t determine the songwriter from the provided summaries. To answer, check the Mindy McCready debut-album track listing and songwriting credits (e.g., the album’s Wikipedia page, liner notes, or reliable music credits databases).

retrieved_doc_snippets: Ten Thousand Angels (song) | "Ten Thousand Angels" is a debut song written by Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on April 1, 1996 as the first single and title track from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached #6 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

I'm Still Here (album) | I'm Still Here is the fifth and final studio album by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released on March 23, 2010, via Linus Entertainment. The album includes the single "I'm Still Here," as well as re-recordings of her 1996 singles "Guys Do It All the Time" and "Ten Thousand Angels." Mindy performed on "Fox & Friends" to promote the album in April 2010. This is McCready's final release before her death in 2013.

Mindy McCready | Malinda Gayle "Mindy" McCready (November 30, 1975 – February 17, 2013) was an American country music singer. Active from 1995 until her death in 2013, she recorded a total of five studio albums. Her debut album, 1996's "Ten Thousand Angels", was released on BNA Records and was certified 2× Platinum by the RIAA, while 1997's "If I Don't Stay the Night" was certified Gold. 1999's "I'm Not So Tough", her final album for BNA, was less successful, and she left the label. A self-title ...[truncated]

Guys Do It All the Time | "Guys Do It All the Time" is a song recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready and written by Bobby Whiteside and Kim Tribble. The song was released on July 15, 1996, and reached the No. 1 spot on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart on September 14 of that year. It is McCready's first and only Number One hit. The song was the second single from McCready's double-platinum selling debut album "Ten Thousand Angels". A video was also issued of t ...[truncated]

Maybe He'll Notice Her Now | "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now" is a song written by Tim Johnson and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready featuring Richie McDonald. It was released on January 6, 1997 as the third single from McCready's double-platinum selling album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 18 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.

Ten Thousand Angels | Ten Thousand Angels is the 1996 debut album of American country music singer Mindy McCready. It was released on BNA Records and sold two million copies being certified double platinum by the RIAA. The album peaked at #5 on the US country charts, and producing four chart singles on the country charts. The first single, which was the title track, reached #6, and was followed by her only #1 hit, "Guys Do It All the Time". Following this song was "Maybe He'll Notice Her Now", w ...[truncated]

A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do) | "A Girl's Gotta Do (What a Girl's Gotta Do)" is a song written by Robert Byrne and Rick Bowles, and recorded by American country music artist Mindy McCready. It was released in February 1997 as the fourth single from the album "Ten Thousand Angels". The song reached number 4 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Singles & Tracks chart.
target_state.summary_2: Mindy McCready, an American country music singer, released the album "I'm Still Here." The debut song on her debut album Ten Thousand Angels was written by Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones.
target_state.answer: Billy Henderson and Steven Dale Jones
